hypothesis: Mutating position 5 to aromatic residues (W, F, Y) will enhance complementarity with SSTR2-specific ECL2/ECL3/TM5/TM6 residues, while mutating position 11 to hydrophobic residues (L, I, V) will reduce off-target (SSTR3/5) binding by avoiding unfavorable electrostatic interactions. This will increase the selectivity margin by differentiating SSTR2 from off-targets.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The hypothesis remains focused on achieving SSTR2 selectivity through disruption of off-target contacts. However, the design must now prioritize mutations at non-pharmacophore positions to push Δmargin further into the positive range.

proposed_changes.rationale: To disrupt conserved-pocket contacts with SSTR3/SSTR5 at non-pharmacophore positions and improve selectivity.
Introduce mutations at non-pharmacophore positions to disrupt conserved pocket interactions with SSTR3/SSTR5 and improve selectivity.
